response: The sequence repeats every five minutes, so three hours yields thirty-six full cycles. Each cycle contains three spins plus two leaps, totaling five moves per cycle. Multiplying thirty-six by five gives one-hundred-eighty total moves performed. Three spins earn four-fifty dollars while two leaps earn five dollars, making eight-fifty per cycle. Thirty-six cycles times eight-fifty equals three-hundred-fifty dollars base income. The growth factor uses time in hours, so we calculate e to the power of zero-point-one-five times three. This yields approximately one-point-fifty-one when rounded. Multiplying three-hundred-fifty by one-point-fifty-one results in five-hundred-three-point-five dollars final earnings.
